claim.statement: Every 3-coloring of {1,...,13} contains either a monochromatic Schur triple x+y=z or a monochromatic 3-term arithmetic progression; equivalently, no 3-coloring of [13] avoids both obstruction families.
claim.negation_of: a 3-coloring of {1,...,13} with no monochromatic Schur triple and no monochromatic 3-term arithmetic progression

rung.name: exhaustive-recomputation
trusted_base: a small Python exhaustive finite-coloring checker (tools/hybrid_schur_vdw_check.py)
the declared hybrid obstruction spec: Schur triples x+y=z and 3-term APs in [n]
a lower-bound witness for [12] checked by the same script

meta.lower_bound_witness_meaning: This 3-coloring of [12] avoids both monochromatic Schur triples and monochromatic 3-term APs, so the certified [13] barrier is tight for this spec.

provenance.pipeline: bounded discovery scout found SAT through n=12 and UNSAT at n=13; live checker re-enumerates the finite obstruction spec and validates the n=12 witness before certifying the n=13 barrier

one_directional: The checker fails closed if the lower-bound witness is invalid or if any counterexample coloring of [13] is found. This is R3, not R2: a bug in the Python exhaustive checker or in the declared hybrid spec could in principle cause a false accept. The claim is a new atlas-certified finite hybrid barrier, not a literature-priority claim.
